best_contact.decision_maker: ## [Be the CMO Everyone Wants to Work With](https://kellblog.com/2026/03/21/how-to-be-the-cmo-everyone-wants-to-work-with/)

best_contact.department: 2026](https://kellblog.com/2026/03/21/how-to-be-the-cmo-everyone-wants-to-work-with/ "7:48 am") | [Leave a comment](https://kellblog.com/2026/03/21/how-to-be-the-cmo-everyone-wants-to-work-with/#respond)

best_contact.decision_maker: I’m a big fan of Dave Gerhardt and the [Exit Five](https://www.exitfive.com/) marketing community he has built. While I have always liked the idea of peer-networking communities
best_contact.department: I think they change from vitamin to painkiller in times of rapid change. Why? Because the playbooks haven’t been written yet.
for example. Today
best_contact.email: you can find scores of blogs — including mine — that talk about how to run SaaS businesses
best_contact.job_title: and how to produce and interpret SaaS metrics. Twenty years ago virtually none of that existed. You needed to figure it out. And one of the best ways to figure it out was to spend time with other people who were doing the same figuring.

best_contact.decision_maker: There’s a time for timeless wisdom and there’s a time for talking to other people who are doing the same thing as you are
best_contact.department: right now. I think AI and the massive disruption it creates in marketing — from content to workflow to performance marketing to analytics — means it’s an awesome time to join a marketing peer-networking community.

best_contact.decision_maker: I tell this to literally every CMO I work with:
* Come to me for timeless wisdom (if not fleeting opinions).
* Join Exit Five (or equivalent) to talk to peers who face the same challenges you do
best_contact.department: every day.

best_contact.decision_maker: That’s not to say that following thought leaders isn’t a great tactic
best_contact.department: too. I keep an eye on Emily Kramer
best_contact.linkedin_url: and Jon Miller. And I can’t wait for Rand Fishkin’s rumored new book on Zero-Click Marketing. If there are other marketing thought leaders you think I should be following

best_contact.decision_maker: All this is why I was thrilled when Dave Gerhardt invited me to speak at Exit Five’s Marketing Leadership Retreat on March 19-20 in Phoenix. Given the above
best_contact.department: I knew I wasn’t going to be the person delivering fresh-from-the-trenches information on AI tools and methods. The audience is 100x more qualified than I am to do that.

best_contact.decision_maker: So what did I want to offer up instead? Some timeless wisdom. Specifically
best_contact.department: timeless wisdom not just on how to successfully do the CMO job
best_contact.email: but on how to be the CMO everyone wants to work with.

best_contact.decision_maker: Why that topic? And bear in mind it takes a lot for me to pick a title that ends with a preposition. Because I thought it captured the key to success in an important way.
* **It’s not just about doing the job**. Yes
best_contact.department: that’s quite hard already but if you only focus on that you ironically increase your odds of becoming a statistic.

best_contact.decision_maker: * **It’s not just about keeping the job**. And yes
best_contact.department: there’s an art to that
best_contact.email: a big part of that is simply remembering to market marketing.

best_contact.decision_maker: * **It’s about helping the boss do their job** which not only increases your strategic value
best_contact.department: but makes you more “sticky” in your role.

best_contact.decision_maker: * It’s about doing all that **while being the CMO that everyone wants to work with** (EW2WW)
Why does that last point matter?

best_contact.department: so a strong [internal customer service](https://kellblog.com/2020/04/26/marketing-exists-to-make-sales-easier/) orientation never hurts.

best_contact.decision_maker: * Being the CMO EW2WW **helps you keep your job**. With a median tenure of 18-24 months
best_contact.department: this should never be too far from a CMO’s mind. Belt and suspenders.

best_contact.decision_maker: * If _everyone_ wants to work with you
best_contact.department: **it helps you find your next job**. Board members and recruiters — your top two job sources after peers — will seek you out when the time comes.

best_contact.decision_maker: * It might well **help you get promoted to COO or CEO**. If everyone wants to work with you
best_contact.department: they might well give you a shot at the next level.

best_contact.decision_maker: * **It frames things as a positioning problem**. And we know a lot about solving positioning problems
best_contact.department: working backwards from a desired result.
I’m saying that CMOs should want to position themselves as the CMO EW2WW.
position thyself.** ”
here are the slides from the presentation
best_contact.full_name: and downloadable [here](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vYxyNiYkkJbL8O2CMzHrP71ubqDNqtAK/view?usp=sharing). I had a fairly miserable time in Gamma building them so apologies for the upside-down funnel
best_contact.phone: the absence of copyright notice and slide numbers). There is only so much time I’m willing to spend explaining to a chatbot what expression to put on the reflected image of a face in a mirror. And once you drop into PowerPoint to make changes
best_contact.seniority: there seems to be no going back.

best_contact.department: with our safe emotional distance from a 1970s auto worker

ride this one to the beach and go retire.
you need to switch horses.

without the emotional distance we have from a 1970s auto worker

best_contact.department: then on that day the BI market will be saturated.

best_contact.decision_maker: But that day strikes me as a long way off. It did 40 years ago and it still does today. We have accomplished so
best_contact.department: so much in BI and analytics. But there is still so much more to do.

best_contact.decision_maker: Since no AI post would be complete without a reference to [Jevons Paradox](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jevons_paradox)

best_contact.linkedin_url: if we had to list the one key to business success

best_contact.decision_maker: It’s not that there’s _only a little_ more work to do. There is an unfathomable amount of work yet to be done. The job of management is pruning that list down to something the organization can actually execute.

# We’re here to propel you forward.
##  [Contact us](https://abadvisors.com/contact/) for a free business assessment!
# [(303) 335-4218](tel:%20303-871-9550)
# Strategic & Management Consulting
**American Business Advisors** (ABA) is the leading business consulting firm in Denver specializing in  _**Building Cash Cows ®**_ and _**Improving Quality of Life ®**_ services for mid-size and small companies, family businesses, and their owners. Established in 1984, we were one of the first Denver consulting firms to offer both management consulting and strategic consulting, and we now offer these services to mid-size and small businesses across America.
# Building Cash Cows®
A cash cow is a business generating steady, dependable cash flow, enabling it to:
  * Fund growth and development
  * Become a market leader
  * Provide discretionary cash flow to owners
  * Determine a predictable business value


# Improving Quality of Life®
Helping business leaders live a balanced life with time for:
  * Family
  * Vacations
  * Personal interests
  * Community and philanthropic involvements


### Who we are and why we do it!
## [SERVICES](https://abadvisors.com/services/)
American Business Advisors provides proven solutions for the issues you face as you navigate the turbulent waters of company growth while managing people, money, markets, and processes. Our proprietary Developmental Consulting Services**™** are designed exclusively to meet the needs of mid-sized companies, small companies, and family businesses. Learn more about our distinctive, proven approach, custom consulting solutions used in our proprietary 14 Dynamics of Building a Cash Cow**™.**
## [Solutions ](https://abadvisors.com/services/#solutions)
## [Our System ](https://abadvisors.com/services/#system)
## [Additional Services ](https://abadvisors.com/services/#additional)
[View All Services](https://abadvisors.com/services)

##  YOUR TRACK
Since 1984, **American Business Advisors** has worked with mid-size and small companies with sales of $1-100 million. Based on unique sets of milestones and challenges, we have identified six tracks into which most businesses can classify themselves. Whether your company is a family business, mid-size business, or small business, ABA works with other companies like yours.
[ Find Your Track ](https://abadvisors.com/find-your-track)

### Business Development Service – ABA The Leading Small Business Consulting Firm in Denver
## Our Purpose
As a Denver small business advisor, ABA provides solutions for the issues faced by mid-size and small businesses as they proceed through turbulent waters while managing people, money, markets, and processes.
## Our Mission
Our passion is to help mid-size and small companies grow and produce wealth, and to enable our staff to maximize their potential. It is our desire that the development of our clients’ wealth and staff potential will result in more humanitarian acts and honor to God.
